records.sourceSnippet: absent of horses. She proposed the creation of a separate agency to manage Nevada’s wild horse and burro populations, separate from the Bureau of Land Management (BLM). Ms. Fuson emphasized that rounding up wild horses and burros and confining them in pens for years was not cost effective. She called for a reassessment of the HMAs and the rewilding of horses to specific areas. Ms. Fuson urged the Board to recognize that supporting the letter would not resolve the wildlife management issues but would instead exacerbate them. She emphasized that the letter from the CHNL was “misrepresented” and “outdated.” Member Boehmer said he appreciated and agreed with some of Ms. Fuson’s points. Supervisor Giomi suggested adding language such as “private property” to temporary use permits, and Ms. Sullivan agreed to it. (8:49:32) – Mayor Bagwell read into the record a proclamation recognizing June 2025 as National Pollinator Month. She also thanked the Chamber of Commerce’s Leadership Classes of 2019 and 2020 for creating the City’s bee habitat at the Carson Tahoe Hospital. Open Space Manager Lyndsey Boyer highlighted the role bees played in our food supply and invited everyone to attend several activities, including the Pollinator Palooza at the Nevada State Museum, and reviewed a list of pollinator-friendly plants. The team joined the Board for a commemorative photograph. 14.c PRESENTATION OF A PROCLAMATION RECOGNIZING JUNE 5, 2025, AS MAYORS' MONARCH

records.sourceSnippet: recused himself. (5:36:32) – Ms. Boyer referenced the Staff Report and Supporting Materials, all of which are incorporated into the record. (5:44:52) – In response to Chair Kimbrough’s question, Ms. Boyer explained that Kings Canyon is a historic road and improvements would be restricted due to preservation concerns. Ms. Boyer added that it remains an emergency access route, though it was not suitable for upgrades. Chair Kimbrough expressed appreciation for the project’s alignment with conservation goals and the protection added by future residential presence.

records.sourceSnippet: absent in recent years. We see only high density apartment complexes for attainable housing. We have young professionals who want single family starter homes which they can trade-up or build a generational home with their parents' help. Any young professional staying in an apartment now is temporary for them and they will move to Dayton or Gardnerville to buy their first home if the current trend to mass building of multi-family homes is not modified. We balanced sustainable growth, not managed growth of the market. Please postpone your approval of land use maps and the Master Plan until the City staff can better explain the changes to residents through public hearings after 5pm or on the weekends. It took the City a year to get here so another couple of months seem only reasonable to explain the changes to residents.. -- 64 of 136 -- 5 Thank you. Bepsy Strasburg and Richard Nagel Res...
